commands.review_summary:
The branch moves session lock, session transcript, and adjacent session-state Doctor repairs into finding-scoped structured health checks with dry-run effects and detect-after validation.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured Doctor repair conversion, not a standalone bug report. Source inspection shows the affected session repair paths, but no current-main failure path is being reported.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer/draft stacked PR needs maintainer contract review plus contributor real behavior proof; no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: Doctor: structure session repair checks
target.body:
## Goal

Move session lock, session transcript, and session state repairs into the structured Doctor repair contract.

This wave converts session maintenance from local repair flows into finding-scoped detection and repair. Existing cleanup behavior stays intact, while dry-run and repair output now describe session `changes`, `warnings`, and `effects` in a machine-readable way.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no runner note arrays.

## Legacy To Modern Shape

Session repair is stateful and user-data-adjacent, so the migration keeps the behavior conservative while making the shape isomorphic:

- `detect()` reports session lock/transcript/state findings without mutation
- `repair()` receives the selected session findings and mutates only those scoped targets
- dry-run describes the session cleanup actions without deleting or rewriting files
- repair effects make cleanup outcomes visible to Doctor preview/reporting
- follow-up detection validates successful non-dry-run repairs

## Converted Here

- session lock repair
- session transcript repair
- session state provider repair wiring
- heartbeat/main-session repair integration
- state integrity checks using structured repair output

## Review Surface

This is the session-policy PR to challenge:

- whether findings should be grouped by session, transcript, lock file, or agent state provider
- whether delete/cleanup effects should expose more target detail for preview and auditability
- whether dry-run output is conservative enough for user-data-adjacent state
- whether detect-after validation covers the right repaired scope

## Stack Position

Parent: #84340 at `7445157ff9`. This PR is the session state wave in the Doctor repair stack.

Follow-on PRs continue the same contract through config audit/cron and the top-level dry-run preview surface.

## Contract Examples In This PR

| Repair area | Detects | Repairs | Dry-run / diff / effect proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Session locks | stale session lock files or lock state selected by the doctor finding | removes only the scoped stale lock targets | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports the lock cleanup target as an effect before deletion; deletes are effects, not fake textual diffs |
| Session transcripts | transcript/session records that need repair or cleanup | runs the existing transcript repair behavior for selected findings | file-backed rewrites can expose diffs where available; cleanup-only work reports effects and warnings without mutating in dry-run |
| Session state providers and heartbeat/main-session repair | stale or inconsistent session state around provider/main-session maintenance | preserves the legacy state repair decisions under finding-scoped repair | dry-run reports planned state cleanup/repair effects before touching user-data-adjacent files, and detect-after validation proves the selected findings were cleared |

Because this area is user-data-adjacent, the table is intentionally conservative: dry-run must identify the target before mutation, `--diff` must only show real file edits, cleanup/delete operations must be effects, and non-dry-run repairs must be followed by detection.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts`
- Fresh PR CI should provide broad proof across the full stack.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Session maintenance repairs now use structured findings, scoped repair, dry-run output, repair effects, and detect-after validation while preserving existing session cleanup behavior.

Real environment tested: Windows isolated worktree at commit `e75bfa2708f0999c52cfa51320df7c07ff986ee5`, restacked on #84340.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-transcripts.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 5 focused session/Doctor test files passed, 80 tests total, covering stale lock detection/removal, transcript repair, dry-run effects, scoped repair, positional contribution ordering, and detect-after validation.

Observed result after fix: Session repair behavior remains available through Doctor, while dry-run and later preview surfaces can inspect what would change before mutation. Notes stay presentation-only.

What was not tested: Live session recovery scenarios and platform-wide Doctor CI were not rerun locally for this body refresh.
